Trigger_Phrases: guaranteed; no risk; can't lose; risk-free; assured return
Rationale: Guarantee/no-risk claims are high-risk marketing statements and can be misleading to clients/prospects.
Example_Flagged_Text: This strategy is guaranteed to produce returns with no risk.
Severity_Guide: High if external; Medium if internal draft

Trigger_Phrases: beat the market; outperform; top-performing; double your money; best-in-class
Rationale: Unsubstantiated performance comparisons or exaggerated claims can be misleading and require strong substantiation and appropriate context.
Example_Flagged_Text: We consistently beat the market and are top-performing in our category.
Severity_Guide: Medium if external; Low-Med if internal

Trigger_Phrases: testimonial; client said; review; 5-star; rating; endorsed; recommended
Rationale: Testimonials/endorsements/ratings create marketing rule risk; need controls, disclosures, and approval workflows.
Example_Flagged_Text: A client review says we are a 5-star firm—let's add it to the deck draft.
Severity_Guide: Medium if external use; Low if only internal discussion

Trigger_Phrases: past performance; track record; returns were; annualized; IRR; gross returns; net returns
Rationale: Performance references often require context and consistency; missing qualifiers can mislead.
Example_Flagged_Text: Past performance shows a 22% annualized return.
Severity_Guide: Medium if external without context; Low if internal draft for review

Trigger_Phrases: WhatsApp; Signal; Telegram; DM; text me; iMessage; call my cell
Rationale: Moving business communications off approved channels can create recordkeeping failures and supervision gaps.
Example_Flagged_Text: Text me on WhatsApp so we don't have to email.
Severity_Guide: High if external/client-related; Medium if internal only

Trigger_Phrases: personal email; gmail; yahoo; send to my personal; non-work email
Rationale: Business communications on personal accounts undermine supervision and retention controls.
Example_Flagged_Text: Send the details to my Gmail so I can review tonight.

Trigger_Phrases: delete this; off the record; don't put in email; wipe; remove this; let's not document
Rationale: Language suggesting concealment or destruction of records indicates serious compliance risk.
Example_Flagged_Text: Off the record—delete this after you read it.

Trigger_Phrases: inside info; insider; non-public; tip; material nonpublic; not public yet
Rationale: Potential MNPI indicators require immediate escalation and containment.
Example_Flagged_Text: I got a non-public tip about their earnings.

Trigger_Phrases: confidential deal; pending acquisition; leaked; embargoed; under NDA
Rationale: Confidential transaction language can imply restricted information or NDA obligations; needs controlled handling.
Example_Flagged_Text: This is under NDA—I'll share details once you're on the call.
Severity_Guide: High if trading/investment context; Medium otherwise

Trigger_Phrases: tickets; gift; dinner; trip; hospitality; entertainment; conference invite
Rationale: Gifts/entertainment can create conflicts of interest and policy threshold issues; requires logging/approval.
Example_Flagged_Text: They offered us tickets and a hotel for the conference.
Severity_Guide: Medium; High if value appears large or quid pro quo implied

Trigger_Phrases: referral fee; kickback; side deal; pay you; finder's fee; commission split
Rationale: Improper compensation arrangements raise conflicts and regulatory risk; must be disclosed/approved.
Example_Flagged_Text: They'll pay us a referral fee if we send clients their way.

Trigger_Phrases: SSN; social security; account number; routing number; wire instructions; passport; driver's license
Rationale: Sharing sensitive personal data in insecure channels is a privacy and cybersecurity risk.
Example_Flagged_Text: Here are the wire instructions and account number—please process ASAP.
Severity_Guide: High if actual sensitive data present; Medium if partial/placeholder
